text: The surgeon performs a crossover dissection, staying high in relatively normal tissue, before completing a contralateral dissection and readjusting the ports for a top-down approach.

The surgeon begins a top-down approach by dissecting around the hernia site, confirming the absence of bowel within the hernia sac. The dissection reveals attenuated muscle and minimal fascia fibers, necessitating reconstruction of the posterior components and careful closure of the anterior components. The closure is successfully achieved despite the challenging tissue conditions.

The surgeon mobilizes the fragile peritoneum along the space of Retzius, performing bilateral lower abdominal transversus abdominis releases (TAR) to achieve closure and provide coverage for the suprapubic defect.
The surgeon anchors the sine core mesh to the pubis using several probing sutures, aiming to maintain mesh integrity and prevent displacement due to the large defect and unhealthy tissue.
